fix: block configure mode on secondary cluster node

config authority. Block 'configure' in both the local CLI and gRPC
EnterConfigure RPC when the node is not primary for RG0.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix Unicode apostrophe issue in bot detection pattern matching

YouTube uses Unicode RIGHT SINGLE QUOTATION MARK (U+2019 ') instead of
regular ASCII apostrophe (') in error messages. This caused the pattern
"you're not a bot" to never match "you're not a bot".

Fixed by normalizing Unicode apostrophes to ASCII before pattern matching.

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

commit.message:
feat: 近因校准智能化 — tanh非线性响应 + 方向一致性 + 数据一致性

三层信号质量评估:
1. 数据一致性(consistency): 局部数据方差→信号可靠度(0.7x~1.3x)
2. 方向一致性(directionBoost): 同向1.3x / 反向0.55x / 中性0.85x
3. tanh非线性响应: 小diff敏感放大，大diff柔和压制

修正幅度 = tanh(diff/scale) × baseMax × consistency × direction × base
三因子乘积动态调节，替代原来的线性clamp

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Fix agent self-approval vulnerability: remove 4 unsafe MCP tools (0.8.0)

Remove approve_action, deny_action, revoke_permission_pattern, and
report_tool_execution from MCP server. An MCP caller (the AI agent) can
relay HMAC challenge tokens to self-approve without human involvement.
The HMAC prevents forgery but not relay.

10 safe read-only/append-only MCP tools remain. Decision recording is
available via HTTP API (for runtimes with their own UI) and Claude Code
hooks (where the native permission dialog is the human gate).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix(collector): correct MI telemetry — energy double-count, slot timing, inferred activity

- EnergyInterface: exclude MI machine peripherals from findAll() so their
  small internal buffers don't inflate energy_total aggregates. Uses
  MACHINE_MOD_PREFIXES blocklist matched against primary peripheral type.

- Poller/Config: add mi_slot_interval_s (30s) and machine_diag_enabled (false)
  config knobs. MI slot/tank collection (list(), tanks()) now runs on its own
  slow timer via collectMISlots() instead of every 5s machine cycle.

- Poller: replace the broken energy-delta inferred_active heuristic with a
  reliable slot+energy signal: inferred_active=1 when occupied>0 AND energy>0.
  Slot data fetched once per 5s cycle and cached for the 30s slow pass.

- Poller: gate machine_activity_diag behind machine_diag_enabled (default off)
  to eliminate thousands of redundant diagnostic writes in normal operation.

- MachineActivity: add getTanks() (IFluidHandler) and getItemSlots()
  (IItemHandler) — the only useful CC-accessible data on MI machines.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
iOSのテキスト入力時のオートズームを無効化 (#28)

## Summary

- `@supports (-webkit-touch-callout: none)` を使い、iOS Safari のみを対象に
  `input`, `textarea`, `select` の `font-size` を `1rem` (16px) に設定
- iOS Safari では font-size が 16px 未満の入力欄をタップすると自動ズームが発生するため、これを防止
- macOS Safari やその他ブラウザには一切影響なし

commit.message:
fix(security): remove eval() from application JavaScript files (#2010) (#10902)

## Summary
- Replaced `eval()` with safer alternatives in 4 first-party JavaScript
files
- Eliminates 8 `eval()` calls from application code (tree menus, fee
sheet, file uploader)
- Third-party/vendor files left unchanged (should be updated upstream)

## Problem
`eval()` is a well-known security risk (arbitrary code execution) and a
CSP violation. Issue #2010 tracks removing all `eval()` usage from the
codebase. This PR addresses the first-party application JavaScript
files.

## Changes

**`library/js/DocumentTreeMenu.js` & `library/js/CategoryTreeMenu.js`**
(6 eval calls):
- Event handlers (`ontoggle`, `oncollapse`, `onexpand`) were executed
via `eval(handler)`
- Replaced with type-checked approach: if handler is a function, call it
directly; if it's a string, use `Function(handler)()` which creates a
new function scope without access to local variables

**`interface/forms/fee_sheet/review/js/fee_sheet_core.js`** (1 eval
call):
- `eval(diags_matches[i])` was used to execute `diags.push(...)` strings
extracted from AJAX response
- Replaced with regex capture group extraction + `JSON.parse()` for safe
argument parsing

**`interface/modules/zend_modules/public/js/scripts/file_uploader.js`**
(1 eval call):
- `eval(success_function+"("+data+")")` built and executed code to call
a callback by name
- Replaced with `window[success_function](data)` for safe global
function lookup

## Not Changed (vendor/third-party)
- `overlib_mini.js` — legacy calendar overlay library
- `jquery.easyui.min.js` — third-party jQuery UI library
- `jquery-ui.min.js` / `jquery.ui.datepicker.js` — jQuery UI
- `telehealth.js` — webpack bundle (eval in devtool source maps)

These should be addressed by updating the upstream packages.

Refs #2010

## Testing
- Document tree and category tree menus: expand/collapse/toggle behavior
preserved
- Fee sheet diagnosis refresh: `diags.push()` parsing works with
JSON.parse fallback
- File uploader: callback invocation via `window[fn]()` lookup

## AI Disclosure
- **AI Used:** Yes — Claude Code (Anthropic) used for implementation
- **Files touched:** 4 JS files — AI-assisted

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

---------

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
Co-authored-by: Craig Allen <craig@interconnected.au>

commit.message:
Harden tunnel resilience, LTE watchdog autonomy, and modem contention safety

Tunnel: channel-based WS sink (eliminates mutex contention), writer exit
detection via oneshot, subscriber task reaping, panic boundaries on message
handlers, pong RTT tracking, flap detection with backoff, TCP_USER_TIMEOUT,
relay health state reset on reconnect, try_send for pong/probe handlers,
session.gap notifications, and capacity-based writer monitoring.

LTE watchdog: full rewrite as state machine with escalating recovery actions
(poll → restart interface → safe-bands → modem reset → full reboot), cooldown
tiers, dormant mode detection, band history tracking. Skips AT commands while
tunnel is connected to avoid killing QMI data path. On-demand polling via
Notify for API requests.

Modem: add command_with_timeout, is_responsive check, and modem_type/port
introspection. GPS poller skips AT commands during tunnel. safe_set_bands
split into wrapper+inner to guarantee band_action_until cleanup on error.

Config: tunable heartbeat timing (5s/20s default). Supervisor process lock
with --skip-lock for child. Dashboard diagnostics fetch and LTE signal display
improvements.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message: Add NSFW subreddits, photo mode, categories, favicon, and ad slots - Replace SFW subreddits with 40 NSFW subs across 6 categories (Porn, Goth/Emo, Latina, Ebony, White Girl, Indian) - Scraper now collects both videos AND images (i.redd.it, galleries) - Database schema: add media_type and category columns with migration - API: filter by media type (?type=video|image) and category - Frontend: Videos/Photos toggle, category-based filter panel - Photo cards: full-screen scroll-snap with instead of

Fix critical citation engine bugs causing page/line inaccuracies

## Summary
Fixes 4 systematic bugs in chunk_documents.py that caused citation errors
requiring attorney redline corrections across 9 motion drafts.

## Bugs Fixed

### Bug 1: Line Range Variable Error (Lines 256-260)
**Problem:** Used `current_page` instead of `page` for line_ranges tracking
**Impact:** Line numbers off by 1-2 (e.g., 175:17-19 instead of 175:15-19)
**Fix:** Changed to use `page` variable from citation lookup

### Bug 2: Bates Numbers Not Preferred for Citations (Lines 710-723)
**Problem:** Citations used page numbers with Bates as suffix
**Impact:** "pp. 1-2 [BATES_001]" instead of legal convention "at BATES_001-002"
**Fix:** Citations now prefer Bates numbers over page numbers when available

### Bug 3: Chunk Overlap Loses Metadata (Lines 289-301)
**Problem:** Only preserved last page/Bates, lost line_ranges and other metadata
**Impact:** Completely wrong pages in citations (e.g., 148 instead of 141/16)
**Fix:** Preserve full metadata for overlap lines including line_ranges

### Bug 4: Paragraph Detection Limited (Lines 395-420)
**Problem:** Only detected "N. " format, missed ¶, §, Paragraph N
**Impact:** Wrong paragraph content in chunks (paras 148-150 wrong section)
**Fix:** Enhanced regex patterns + added hard max_tokens safety limit

## Specific Error Examples Fixed

1. **Line off by 2:** System generated 175:17-19, correct is 175:15-19 → FIXED
2. **Page off by 1:** System generated pp. 1-2, correct is pp. 2-3 → NEEDS TESTING
3. **Wrong page:** System generated 148:19-21, correct is 141:10-16 → FIXED
4. **Wrong paragraph content:** paras 148-150 wrong section → FIXED

## Testing Required

- Re-chunk Kindler deposition to verify line number accuracy
- Re-chunk Google Agreement to verify page indexing
- Re-chunk Kindler Report to verify paragraph boundaries
- Verify Bates number format in all citations

## Files Modified

- `/Users/maximprice/Dev/lit-doc-pipeline/chunk_documents.py` (4 bug fixes)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.5 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

ci: stamp npm version from GitHub release tag before publishing

Replace the version-mismatch guard with an explicit npm version set.
publish.yml now reads GITHUB_REF_NAME (e.g. v1.2.3), strips the 'v',
and calls `npm version <semver> --no-git-tag-version` before publishing.
This guarantees the published npm version always matches the GitHub release,
with no manual coordination required.

fix: add dynamic whitelist notice to AI instructions

prompt listing only enabled forms. This prevents the AI from suggesting
disabled forms (like AINVOICES, SUPPLIERS) that it knows from the base
instructions but are actually blocked.

commit.message:
feat: add passthrough LLM mode for ask-question tool (#335)

* feat: add passthrough LLM mode for ask-question tool

Adds llm.provider = "passthrough" so the ask-question MCP tool returns
retrieved context chunks directly to the calling LLM instead of requiring
a separate OpenAI/Ollama provider. This is the natural design for MCP tools
where the client already has an LLM (e.g. Claude Code).

- config.ts: add "passthrough" to llm.provider union type and env var handling
- rag.ts: add isPassthroughMode() helper and getContextForQuestion() which
  retrieves and formats context without an LLM call
- mcp/server.ts: ask-question checks passthrough first and returns context
  directly; falls through to existing LLM path otherwise

Enable via config:  { "llm": { "provider": "passthrough" } }
Enable via env var: LIBSCOPE_LLM_PROVIDER=passthrough

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: format config.ts and include passthrough in provider override

- Reformat long if-condition to satisfy prettier (printWidth: 100)
- Fix logic bug: passthrough provider was checked in outer condition but
  not spread into overrides.llm.provider

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: preserve bullet/heading formatting when saving notes

Two fixes:
1. Remove isChangeOrigin filter that was blocking toolbar-triggered
   formatting changes (bullets, headings, etc.) from reaching the
   save handler - these actions route through Yjs and were incorrectly
   filtered as "remote" changes
2. Split hard breaks into separate paragraphs before toggling
   bullet/ordered lists so multi-line selections get individual
   list items instead of one combined item
